name: Agent-First Config
synopsis: Generic dot-path accessor for JSON/TOML/YAML configs, usable both as a **Rust library** and as a standalone **`afconfig` CLI binary**. The library is the primary interface: spores embed it to get `get_key`/`set_key` without writing a dispatch table per field. The CLI wraps the same logic for shell scripts and quick one-off edits from any language.
intent: Provide a reusable config traversal and mutation library that works with any serde struct
Enable dot-path get/set operations (e.g., imap.host, identities.me.email) without enumeration in Clap
Support slug-based array access (keyed lists) alongside nested object and scalar paths
Keep the lib lightweight with zero format dependencies; format backends via feature flags
Solve the consistency problem in multi-format tools where config CLI patterns diverge
mutations: Provide dot-path get/set/add/remove for JSON, TOML, and YAML config files via both a Rust library and a standalone afconfig CLI binary
Route keyed-list arrays by element slug (e.g. identities.me.email) and by numeric index (e.g. steps.0.move_to_mailbox_id), with numeric index taking priority
Accept an optional seed template in add_keyed so new keyed-list entries inherit default field values, overridden by explicit fields
Simplify add_scalar, remove_scalar, and remove_path by removing the unused keyed_lists parameter from their signatures
Expose CliSchema trait for generating markdown reference docs and annotated TOML/YAML config files from field definitions
Coerce CLI string arguments to typed values by shape with s:, b:, i:, j: prefix overrides for forced types
